title: Remind me on a schedule
problem: A message arrives in your Telegram at the time you choose. Water the plants, stretch, take the medicine — the simplest automation there is, and a good first one.

params.label: Schedule
params.help: 5 fields: minute hour day month weekday — "0 9 * * *" means every day at 9:00 (server time)

params.label: Message text
params.help: Sent to Telegram exactly as written

params.label: Telegram chat
params.help: uses ${TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID} from server env — nothing to enter

title: Watch a number from any API
problem: Exchange rates, prices, temperatures, follower counts — if it's a number in a public API, Potok can watch it. You get exactly one message when the number crosses your line, and silence while nothing changes.

params.label: Which number to watch
params.help: Path to the number inside the response, e.g. $.price or $.stats.followers

params.label: Alert when the value is
params.help: How to compare the value with your threshold

params.label: Threshold
params.help: The line that triggers the message

title: Get told when a page mentions something
problem: Waiting for a name to appear in results, a city added to a tour, your street in a roadworks notice? Potok reads the page for you and sends one message the moment the words show up.

params.label: Word or phrase to wait for

title: Know when a price drops
problem: Pick the price on any shop page and name your limit. Potok reads the price tag — "249,99 zł" is fine — and messages you once when it falls under your number.

params.label: Product page address
params.help: The page with the price on it

params.label: Where the price is
params.help: CSS selector of the price element — right-click the price in your browser → Inspect

title: Never miss a recurring payment
problem: Rent, insurance, the subscription you keep forgetting about. One message on the same day every month, before the deadline instead of after it.
sample: 💳 Rent is due today — transfer before 18:00

params.label: Day of the month
params.help: 1–28, so it works in February too

params.label: Message text
params.default: 💳 Rent is due today — transfer before 18:00
params.help: Sent to Telegram exactly as written

title: Get told when your site is down
problem: Find out from a Telegram message, not from your users. Potok knocks on your site every few minutes and alerts you the moment it answers with an error.

params.label: Address to check
params.help: A page that should always answer OK

title: Know when a project ships a new release
problem: Following a tool or library you depend on? Every new release becomes one Telegram message with the version and a link — no GitHub account settings, no emails to dig through.
sample: 🚀 New release in spring-projects/spring-boot: v3.5.14 — https://github.com/…

params.label: GitHub project
params.help: owner/name, exactly as in the project's address

title: Follow a feed in Telegram
problem: Any news feed, blog or podcast that publishes RSS lands in your chat — one message per new item, each item delivered exactly once.
sample:
📰 Show HN: Potok — workflow engine without a broker
https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=…

params.label: Feed address
params.help: RSS or Atom — most news sites have one

title: Know the moment a page changes
problem: Sold-out product, closed registration, "no slots available" — pick the one spot on the page that says so, and get pinged the moment it says something else. Ads and timestamps elsewhere on the page never trigger false alarms.
sample: 👀 The page changed: In stock — https://shop.example.com/product/123

params.label: Which part of the page
params.help: CSS selector — right-click the element in your browser → Inspect

title: Buy euros when they're cheap
problem: The Polish central bank publishes the official exchange rate every day, for free. Set the rate you're waiting for and get one message when the euro dips under it.
sample: 💶 EUR is at 4.1850 — below your 4.20 threshold, good time to exchange

params.label: Rates address
params.help: NBP's free API — swap eur for usd, chf, gbp…

params.label: Which number to watch
params.help: Path to the rate inside the response — the default fits NBP

params.label: Alert below
params.help: One message when the rate drops under this

params.label: Check every
params.help: The bank updates once a day — hourly is plenty

title: Renew certificates before they expire
problem: An expired certificate takes your site down with a scary browser warning — and it always happens on a weekend. A daily check tells you two weeks ahead instead.
sample: 🔒 Certificate for example.com expires in 13 days (2026-06-25T12:00:00Z)

params.label: Warn this many days ahead
params.help: Quiet until fewer days remain than this

title: See repo pushes in Telegram
problem: Every push to your repository becomes a chat message: who pushed and what. Deliveries are cryptographically signed, so nobody can fake them.
sample: ⬆️ yura -> AntonovYuriy/Potok: fix: validate conditions at create time

params.label: Webhook path
params.help: GitHub will send updates to https://your-host/hooks/<path>

params.label: Secret env var NAME
params.help: Name of the server env var holding the shared secret — set the same value in GitHub webhook settings; the secret itself is never typed here

title: Remember to put the bins out
problem: Checks the official Warsaw waste schedule every morning and sends one line when something is collected today — silence otherwise. Also the example of a custom action: a city-specific API wrapped in ~100 lines of Java.

params.label: Address point id
params.help: From the address autocomplete on warszawa19115.pl

params.label: Schedule
params.help: Morning check, server time (compose sets TZ=Europe/Warsaw)
